# Content Type Templates

This file contains structure templates for specific educational content types. Read the relevant section before writing.

---

## 1. Concept Explanation Article

Use when: teaching a new idea, term, or principle for the first time.

```
Hook (scenario or question)
Learning Objectives
What is [Concept]? (plain language definition)
Why does it matter? (motivation + real-world relevance)
How does it work? (mechanics, broken into sections)
  - Core behavior
  - Key rules or constraints
  - Visual/code example
Common misconceptions
Summary
What's Next
```

---

## 2. How-To / Tutorial Article

Use when: teaching a process or skill the learner will perform step by step.

```
Hook (problem this solves)
Learning Objectives
Prerequisites (what the learner must already know)
Step 1: [Action verb + what]
  - Explanation
  - Code/example
  - Expected result
Step 2: ...
(repeat for all steps)
Full working example (end-to-end)
Troubleshooting common errors
Summary
What's Next
```

---

## 3. Comparison Article

Use when: explaining the difference between two or more similar things.

```
Hook (when would you choose one over the other?)
Learning Objectives
Brief intro to both options
Comparison table (key attributes side by side)
When to use [Option A]
When to use [Option B]
Decision guide (simple flowchart or rules)
Summary
What's Next
```

---

## 4. Module Introduction

Use when: opening a new module in the course.

```
What this module is about (1–2 sentences)
Why this module matters (relevance and motivation)
What you'll learn (bullet list of lesson topics)
What you'll be able to do by the end (skills)
Any prerequisites
Quick overview of the module structure
Encouraging closing line
```

---

## 5. Lesson Summary / Recap

Use when: closing a lesson or providing a standalone review.

```
What we covered (3–5 bullet points)
Key terms introduced (term + one-line definition each)
Key takeaways (the 1–3 most important ideas)
Review questions (2–3 comprehension questions)
Bridge to next lesson
```

---

## 6. Case Study / Real-World Example

Use when: showing how a concept applies in practice.

```
Hook (the real situation)
Context (who, what, why)
The challenge or problem
How [concept/tool] was applied
What happened (outcome)
What this teaches us
Reflection question for the learner
```
